Construction of United States Embassy in Ottawa, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–246, title I, § 125104 Stat. 27Feb. 16, 1990“Section 402(a) of the Diplomatic Security Act () shall not apply to the construction or renovation of the United States Embassy in Ottawa, Canada.”22 U.S.C. 4852(a)

children.heading: Preference for United States contractors
children.label: a Preference for United States contractors

children.text: a diplomatic construction or design project which has an estimated total project value exceeding $25,000,000; and
a diplomatic construction or design project which involves technical security, unless the project involves low-level technology, as determined by the Secretary of State.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: b Exception
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply with respect to any diplomatic construction or design project in a foreign country whose statutes prohibit the use of United States contractors on such projects. The exception contained in this subsection shall only become effective with respect to a foreign country 30 days after the Secretary of State certifies to the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate what specific actions he has taken to urge such foreign country to permit the use of United States contractors on such projects, and what actions he shall take with respect to that country as authorized by title II of the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 ( et seq.; commonly referred to as the “Foreign Missions Act”).22 U.S.C. 4301
the term “adequate competition” means with respect to a construction or design project, the presence of three or more qualified bidders submitting responsive bids for that project;

children.label: A
children.text: is incorporated or legally organized under the laws of the United States, including State, the District of Columbia, and local laws;

children.label: B
children.text: has its principal place of business in the United States;

children.label: i
children.text: for more than 5 years before the issuance date of the invitation for bids or request for proposals with respect to a construction project under subsection (a)(1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: for more than 2 years before the issuance date of the invitation for bids or request for proposals with respect to a construction or design project which involves physical or technical security under subsection (a)(2);

children.label: D
children.text: has performed within the United States or on a Federal contract abroad administrative and technical, professional, or construction services similar in complexity, type of construction, and value to the project being bid;

children.label: i
children.text: employs United States citizens in at least 65 percent of its principal management positions in the United States,

children.label: ii
children.text: employs United States citizens in more than half of its permanent, full-time positions in the United States, and

children.label: iii
children.text: will employ United States citizens in at least 65 percent of the supervisory positions on the foreign buildings office project site; and
the term “qualified United States joint venture person” means a joint venture in which a United States person or persons owns at least 51 percent of the assets of the joint venture.

children.heading: American minority contractors
children.label: d American minority contractors
children.text: Not less than 10 percent of the amount appropriated pursuant to for diplomatic construction or design projects each fiscal year shall be allocated to the extent practicable for contracts with American minority contractors.section 4851(a) of this title

children.heading: American small business contractors
children.label: e American small business contractors
children.text: Not less than 10 percent of the amount appropriated pursuant to for diplomatic construction or design projects each fiscal year shall be allocated to the extent practicable for contracts with American small business contractors.section 4851(a) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on subcontracting
children.label: f Limitation on subcontracting
children.text: With respect to a diplomatic construction project, a prime contractor may not subcontract more than 50 percent of the total value of its contract for that project.
